steps.title: Use the existing intersection
steps.content: The original two-line system has exactly one solution: the point where the two shown lines intersect. From the graph, that intersection is $(8,2)$.

steps.title: Test the third equation
steps.content: For the three-equation system to have a solution, the original intersection point must also satisfy the new equation $x+4y=-16$. Substitute $(8,2)$: $$8+4(2)=16$$

steps.title: Compare to the required value
steps.content: The new equation requires $x+4y=-16$, but the original intersection gives $16$. Since $(8,2)$ is not on the third line, no point can satisfy all three equations.

steps.title: State the result
steps.content: The resulting system has zero solutions, which matches Choice A — Zero.
